title: paepalanthus affinis
intro: Paepalanthus affinis, also known as the "Brazilian sandmat," is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. This plant is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, and is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Paepalanthus affinis are thought to include antioxidant and immune-boosting effects, as well as potential anti-cancer and anti-diabetic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically dried and used to make teas, infusions, or infuse in oils for topical application.
